prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `80214e03`.
codex_response:
Implemented `TASK_ID 80214e03` end-to-end. The rule is: extract the coarse row and column bands from the maximal foreground panel bounding boxes, take the dominant nonzero color in each induced region, and return the resulting color grid with columns reversed right-to-left. The summary hint was consistent with the official examples, so I used it; no `notes.md` was needed.

The task package is [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_80214e03/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_80214e03/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_80214e03/generator.py). The generator builds a small coarse grid of row/column bands, merges some cells into rectangular horizontal or vertical bars to match the family, renders them as large holey panels on a zero background, and keeps sizes in the observed `18-22` by `11-19` range. No shared primitive was added.

Verification/build results: official verifier passed `4/4`, the shared builder produced `1000` examples, `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task 80214e03` passed `1000/1000`, and `preview_01.png` looked on-family on visual inspection.
